content.problemStatement:
You are given a **binary string** SSS of size NNN, and can perform the following operation on it:


Pick 333 integers i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k such that 1‚Č§i<j<k‚Č§‚ą£S‚ą£1 \le i < j < k \le |S|1‚Č§i<j<k‚Č§‚ą£S‚ą£ and Si‚Č†Sj,Sj‚Č†SkS_i \ne S_j, S_j \ne S_kSi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Sj‚Äč,Sj‚ÄčÓÄ†=Sk‚Äč, then delete SjS_jSj‚Äč from the string (concatenating the parts formed).
Find the minimum possible length of the string after performing some number (possibly 000) of operations on it.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the length of the string SSS.
The second line contains SSS - a binary string of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum final length of the binary string SSS.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

Test Case 2 : No operations are possible.
